prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_DWT_catboost', seasonality_champion='ETS', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The analysis reveals significant model disagreement (RMSE spread ratio: 1.38) and high seasonal capture variance (1.23), with different models excelling at trend tracking (ONLY_DWT_catboost) versus seasonality (ETS). Early horizons favor ETS while late horizons prefer DWT_catboost, supporting a DBA combination approach that leverages diverse model strengths to reduce overall error.

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_DWT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "ETS",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_CWT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ETS", "late": "DWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "catboost", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "CWT_svr", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "ETS"],
  "tier2_models": ["CWT_catboost", "DWT_rf", "FT_catboost", "rf", "DWT_catboost", "FT_rf", "FT_svr", "CWT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.3766,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The analysis reveals significant model disagreement (RMSE spread ratio: 1.38) and high seasonal capture variance (1.23), with different models excelling at trend tracking (ONLY_DWT_catboost) versus seasonality (ETS). Early horizons favor ETS while late horizons prefer DWT_catboost, supporting a DBA combination approach that leverages diverse model strengths to reduce overall error."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No redundancy issues (max 2 of any type). All candidates appear safe from leakage (baselines never leak; selection/weighted use past windows via anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection per eval_config.rolling.mode='expanding'). Universe leaderboards show topk_mean_per_horizon_k6 and inverse_rmse_weights_k6 variants as strong performers, but current set already has diverse types and sufficient candidates. No changes needed.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["topk_mean_per_horizon_k6", "inverse_rmse_weights_k6_sh0.70"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "n_windows=3 is low, so recommended_knobs suggest stronger regularization. Universe shows k6 variants outperform k5: topk_mean_per_horizon_k6 has RMSE=22683 vs current 22750; inverse_rmse_weights_k6_sh0.70 has RMSE=22659. Adding these improves performance while maintaining diversity. Current set already has good method coverage (selection, weighted, baseline).", "changes": ["added topk_mean_per_horizon_k6 for better k selection with few windows", "added inverse_rmse_weights_k6_sh0.70 for stronger shrinkage with low n_windows"], "when_good": "n_windows >= 6 would allow ridge_stacking to become viable"}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The analysis reveals significant model disagreement (RMSE spread ratio: 1.38) and high seasonal capture variance (1.23), with different models excelling at trend tracking (ONLY_DWT_catboost) versus seasonality (ETS). Early horizons favor ETS while late horizons prefer DWT_catboost, supporting a DBA combination approach that leverages diverse model strengths to reduce overall error.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=7 indicates high model diversity across horizons. RMSE spread_ratio=1.38 suggests significant model disagreement, favoring dba_combination (recommended by pattern_analyst_insights) and inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. With n_windows=3, we need conservative top_k=5 to avoid overfitting. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific strengths. ETS is seasonality_champion while ONLY_DWT_catboost is trend_champion - dba_combination leverages both.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No redundancy issues (max 2 of any type). All candidates appear safe from leakage (baselines never leak; selection/weighted use past windows via anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection per eval_config.rolling.mode='expanding'). Universe leaderboards show topk_mean_per_horizon_k6 and inverse_rmse_weights_k6 variants as strong performers, but current set already has diverse types and sufficient candidates. No changes needed.

statistician.rationale: n_windows=3 is low, so recommended_knobs suggest stronger regularization. Universe shows k6 variants outperform k5: topk_mean_per_horizon_k6 has RMSE=22683 vs current 22750; inverse_rmse_weights_k6_sh0.70 has RMSE=22659. Adding these improves performance while maintaining diversity. Current set already has good method coverage (selection, weighted, baseline).
changes: added topk_mean_per_horizon_k6 for better k selection with few windows
added inverse_rmse_weights_k6_sh0.70 for stronger shrinkage with low n_windows
statistician.when_good: n_windows >= 6 would allow ridge_stacking to become viable
